hero.headline: Teach English that students are finally brave enough to speak.
hero.subheadline: Bridges Institute brings English-speaking teachers into schools across the Negev to lead active speaking lessons — movement, games, stories, role-play and real conversation.
hero.imageAlt: A teacher leading an active English speaking lesson with students in the Negev

trust.gefen: Recognized by the Israeli Ministry of Education, GEFEN Program No. {{program}}

why.title: Meaningful work, with real support behind it.

meaning.title: Real meaning
meaning.body: You help kids who freeze up in English actually open their mouths.

active.title: Active classrooms
active.body: You're on your feet: games, stories, role-play, movement. Not reading from a textbook.

support.title: Support and structure
support.body: Teachers get lesson frameworks, activities and routines. You bring the energy; we provide the structure.

personality.title: Room for personality
personality.body: Warmth, presence and reliability matter here. Teachers bring themselves into the room.

job.title: A lesson at Bridges, step by step.
job.quote: Confidence first. Accuracy follows. Students speak first; we polish later.
flow: Students first listen and respond with their bodies before they're asked to speak.
They repeat useful words and phrases out loud, together.
Then they play, act, choose, answer and talk.
Younger students: TPR, pictures, objects, rhythm, movement, stories.
Older students: opinions, role-play, interviews, real-life situations, conversation tasks.
Every student leaves the room having used English out loud.

fit.title: An honest picture — so you can tell if this is you.
fit.caption: Who is a good fit to teach with Bridges, and who is probably not.

rows.good: Comfortable leading a room of children or teens
rows.bad: Dislikes movement, noise, games or group energy
Uncomfortable leading and managing a class

rows.good: Enjoys active teaching — speaking, movement, games
Understands many students are nervous and need confidence first
rows.bad: Only wants to teach grammar from a book

requirements.title: What you need to apply
requirements.intro: The questions people ask most — answered honestly. One detail below is still being finalised.
requirements.noHebrewBody: Lessons run in English — you don't need Hebrew to teach with Bridges.
requirements.qualsTitle: No degree or teaching certificate required.
requirements.qualsBody: We welcome fluent, capable, energetic people who love working with children. Teaching experience is a plus, not a must.
requirements.locationNote: Which Negev towns/areas, and is your own transport needed? (open-questions A4)
requirements.paid: This is paid work, not volunteering.

photos.title: Real lessons, in real Negev schools.
photos.caption: Caption to confirm with the client.
alt: A teacher leading young students in an active floor game with balloons and letter cards
A teacher leading a speaking lesson at the whiteboard as students follow along
A Bridges teacher and students smiling together during a small-group lesson
A teacher and students around a table playing a picture-card vocabulary game
A teacher helping a student one-on-one at a laptop in the school library
photos.videoNote: If a real clip exists, embed it here with captions/transcript. Asset from client (open-questions B2).

apply.closing: Ready to teach with Bridges? Help students in the Negev find their voice in English.

schools.body: Bridges Institute brings English-speaking teachers into schools and learning centers across the Negev. Our programs help students build spoken English confidence through active, communication-based lessons.
schools.gefen: Recognized under the Israeli Ministry of Education's GEFEN framework, Program No. 9003.

meta.title: Teach English in the Negev — Bridges Institute
meta.description: Bridges Institute hires English-speaking teachers to lead active speaking lessons in schools across the Negev — movement, games, stories, role-play and real conversation. Apply to teach.
